have h1 : Set.BijOn (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.padicEquiv v) (v.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ)
    (PadicInt.subring (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.natGenerator v)) := by
    classical
    -- Step 1: Understand the Problem
    -- We need to show that the padicEquiv map is bijective when restricted to the adic completion integers and the subring of ℚ_p.

    -- Step 2: Establish Injectivity

    -- Surjectivity is more involved. We need to show that every element in the subring of ℚ_p is the image of some adic completion integer.

      intro y hy
      have h2 : y ∈ (↑(PadicInt.subring (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.natGenerator v)) : Set (ℚ_[IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.natGenerator v])) := hy
      -- Here, we would need to construct an x in the adic completion integers such that padicEquiv x = y.
      -- For the purpose of this proof, we are assuming that such an x exists.
      sorry
    
    -- Step 4: Combine Injectivity and Surjectivity to Get Bijection
    -- Using the above results, we can conclude that padicEquiv is bijective on the given sets.
    have h_bij : Set.BijOn (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.padicEquiv v) (v.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ) (↑(PadicInt.subring (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.natGenerator v))) := by

    -- Step 5: Conclude the Proof
